children.text: the underpayment rate established under section 6621,
to the amount of the underpayment,
for the period of the underpayment.

children.heading: Amount of underpayment; period of underpayment
children.label: b Amount of underpayment; period of underpayment
A
children.text: the required installment, over

children.label: B
children.text: the amount (if any) of the installment paid on or before the due date for the installment.

children.label: A
children.text: the 15th day of the 4th month following the close of the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to any portion of the underpayment, the date on which such portion is paid.

children.heading: Order of crediting payments
children.label: 3 Order of crediting payments
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(B), a payment of estimated tax shall be credited against unpaid required installments in the order in which such installments are required to be paid.

children.heading: Number of required installments; due dates
children.label: c Number of required installments; due dates

children.heading: Payable in 4 installments
children.label: 1 Payable in 4 installments
children.text: There shall be 4 required installments for each taxable year.

children.heading: Time for payment of installments
children.label: 2 Time for payment of installments
children.text: In the case of the followingrequired installments:The due date is:1stApril 152ndJune 153rdSeptember 154thJanuary 15 of the following taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amount of any required installment shall be 25 percent of the required annual payment.

children.label: i
children.text: 90 percent of the tax shown on the return for the taxable year (or, if no return is filed, 90 percent of the tax for such year), or

children.label: ii
children.text: 100 percent of the tax shown on the return of the individual for the preceding taxable year.

children.heading: Limitation on use of preceding year’s tax
children.label: C Limitation on use of preceding year’s tax

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If the adjusted gross income shown on the return of the individual for the preceding taxable year beginning in any calendar year exceeds $150,000, clause (ii) of subparagraph (B) shall be applied by substituting “110 percent” for “100 percent”.

children.heading: Separate returns
children.label: ii Separate returns
children.text: In the case of a married individual (within the meaning of section 7703) who files a separate return for the taxable year for which the amount of the installment is being determined, clause (i) shall be applied by substituting “$75,000” for “$150,000”.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: iii Special rule
children.text: In the case of an estate or trust, adjusted gross income shall be determined as provided in section 67(e).

children.heading: Lower required installment where annualized income installment is less than amount determined under paragraph (1)
children.label: 2 Lower required installment where annualized income installment is less than amount determined under paragraph (1)
i
children.text: the amount of such required installment shall be the annualized income installment, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any reduction in a required installment resulting from the application of this subparagraph shall be recaptured by increasing the amount of the next required installment determined under paragraph (1) by the amount of such reduction (and by increasing subsequent required installments to the extent that the reduction has not previously been recaptured under this clause).

children.heading: Determination of annualized income installment
children.label: B Determination of annualized income installment
i
children.text: an amount equal to the applicable percentage of the tax for the taxable year computed by placing on an annualized basis the taxable income, alternative minimum taxable income, and adjusted self-employment income for months in the taxable year ending before the due date for the installment, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount of any prior required installments for the taxable year.

children.heading: Annualization
children.label: i Annualization
children.text: The taxable income, alternative minimum taxable income, and adjusted self-employment income shall be placed on an annualized basis under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: ii Applicable percentage
children.text: In the case of the followingrequired installments:The applicable percentage is:1st22.52nd453rd67.54th90.

children.heading: Adjusted self-employment income
children.label: iii Adjusted self-employment income
children.text: The term “adjusted self-employment income” means self-employment income (as defined in section 1402(b)); except that section 1402(b) shall be applied by placing wages (within the meaning of section 1402(b)) for months in the taxable year ending before the due date for the installment on an annualized basis consistent with clause (i).

children.heading: Treatment of subpart F income
children.label: D Treatment of subpart F income

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any amounts required to be included in gross income under section 951(a) (and credits properly allocable thereto) shall be taken into account in computing any annualized income installment under subparagraph (B) in a manner similar to the manner under which partnership income inclusions (and credits properly allocable thereto) are taken into account.

children.label: I
children.text: clause (i) shall not apply, and

children.label: II
children.text: for purposes of computing any annualized income installment for such taxable year, the taxpayer shall be treated as having received ratably during such taxable year items of income and credit described in clause (i) in an amount equal to the amount of such items shown on the return of the taxpayer for the preceding taxable year (the second preceding taxable year in the case of the first and second required installments for such taxable year).

children.heading: Where tax is small amount
children.label: 1 Where tax is small amount
children.text: No addition to tax shall be imposed under subsection (a) for any taxable year if the tax shown on the return for such taxable year (or, if no return is filed, the tax), reduced by the credit allowable under section 31, is less than $1,000.

children.heading: Where no tax liability for preceding taxable year
children.label: 2 Where no tax liability for preceding taxable year
A
children.text: the preceding taxable year was a taxable year of 12 months,

children.label: B
children.text: the individual did not have any liability for tax for the preceding taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: the individual was a citizen or resident of the United States throughout the preceding taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No addition to tax shall be imposed under subsection (a) with respect to any underpayment to the extent the Secretary determines that by reason of casualty, disaster, or other unusual circumstances the imposition of such addition to tax would be against equity and good conscience.

children.heading: Newly retired or disabled individuals
children.label: B Newly retired or disabled individuals
I
children.text: retired after having attained age 62, or

children.label: II
children.text: became disabled,

children.label: ii
children.text: such underpayment was due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect.

children.heading: Tax computed after application of credits against tax
children.label: f Tax computed after application of credits against tax

children.text: the tax imposed by chapter 1 (other than any increase in such tax by reason of section 143(m)), plus
the tax imposed by chapter 2, plus
the tax imposed by chapter 2A, minus
the credits against tax provided by part IV of subchapter A of chapter 1, other than the credit against tax provided by section 31 (relating to tax withheld on wages).

children.heading: Application of section in case of tax withheld on wages
children.label: g Application of section in case of tax withheld on wages

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of applying this section, the amount of the credit allowed under section 31 for the taxable year shall be deemed a payment of estimated tax, and an equal part of such amount shall be deemed paid on each due date for such taxable year, unless the taxpayer establishes the dates on which all amounts were actually withheld, in which case the amounts so withheld shall be deemed payments of estimated tax on the dates on which such amounts were actually withheld.

children.label: A
children.text: wage withholding, and

children.label: B
children.text: all other amounts withheld for which credit is allowed under section 31.

children.heading: Special rule where return filed on or before January 31
children.label: h Special rule where return filed on or before January 31
children.text: If, on or before January 31 of the following taxable year, the taxpayer files a return for the taxable year and pays in full the amount computed on the return as payable, then no addition to tax shall be imposed under subsection (a) with respect to any underpayment of the 4th required installment for the taxable year.

children.heading: Special rules for farmers and fishermen
children.label: i Special rules for farmers and fishermen
A
children.text: there shall be only 1 required installment for the taxable year,

children.label: B
children.text: the due date for such installment shall be January 15 of the following taxable year,

children.label: C
children.text: the amount of such installment shall be equal to the required annual payment determined under subsection (d)(1)(B) by substituting “66⅔ percent” for “90 percent” and without regard to subparagraph (C) of subsection (d)(1), and

children.label: i
children.text: by substituting “March 1” for “January 31”, and

children.label: ii
children.text: by treating the required installment described in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph as the 4th required installment.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual’s gross income from farming or fishing (including oyster farming) for the taxable year is at least 66⅔ percent of the total gross income from all sources for the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: such individual’s gross income from farming or fishing (including oyster farming) shown on the return of the individual for the preceding taxable year is at least 66⅔ percent of the total gross income from all sources shown on such return.

children.heading: Special rules for nonresident aliens
children.label: j Special rules for nonresident aliens

children.heading: Payable in 3 installments
children.label: 1 Payable in 3 installments
children.text: There shall be 3 required installments for the taxable year.

children.heading: Time for payment of installments
children.label: 2 Time for payment of installments
children.text: The due dates for required installments under this subsection shall be determined under the following table:In the case of the followingrequired installments:The due date is:1stJune 152ndSeptember 153rdJanuary 15 of the following taxable year.

children.heading: First required installment
children.label: A First required installment
children.text: In the case of the first required installment, subsection (d) shall be applied by substituting “50 percent” for “25 percent” in subsection (d)(1)(A).

children.heading: Determination of applicable percentage
children.label: B Determination of applicable percentage
children.text: The applicable percentage for purposes of subsection (d)(2) shall be determined under the following table:In the case of the followingrequired installments:The applicable percentage is:1st22.52nd453rd67.54th90.

children.heading: Fiscal years and short years
children.label: k Fiscal years and short years

children.heading: Fiscal years
children.label: 1 Fiscal years
children.text: In applying this section to a taxable year beginning on any date other than January 1, there shall be substituted, for the months specified in this section, the months which correspond thereto.

children.heading: Short taxable year
children.label: 2 Short taxable year
children.text: This section shall be applied to taxable years of less than 12 months in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, this section shall apply to any estate or trust.

children.heading: Exception for estates and certain trusts
children.label: 2 Exception for estates and certain trusts
A
children.text: the estate of such decedent, or

children.label: i
children.text: all of which was treated (under subpart E of part I of subchapter J of chapter 1) as owned by the decedent, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to which the residue of the decedent’s estate will pass under his will (or, if no will is admitted to probate, which is the trust primarily responsible for paying debts, taxes, and expenses of administration).

children.heading: Exception for charitable trusts and private foundations
children.label: 3 Exception for charitable trusts and private foundations
children.text: This section shall not apply to any trust which is subject to the tax imposed by section 511 or which is a private foundation.

children.heading: Special rule for annualizations
children.label: 4 Special rule for annualizations
children.text: In the case of any estate or trust to which this section applies, subsection (d)(2)(B)(i) shall be applied by substituting “ending before the date 1 month before the due date for the installment” for “ending before the due date for the installment”.

children.heading: Special rule for Medicare tax
children.label: m Special rule for Medicare tax
children.text: For purposes of this section, the tax imposed under section 3101(b)(2) (to the extent not withheld) shall be treated as a tax imposed under chapter 2.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: n Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.
